commands.review_summary:
The PR changes ACP final_only projection to retain accumulated final text and output-limit state across intermediate done/error resets and adds projector regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main clears finalOnlyOutputText and output-limit state on each done/error while dispatch-acp performs a later turn-level flush. I did not run tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real behavior proof and maintainer consolidation with duplicate ACP branches, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(acp): preserve final_only text across tool-call boundaries
target.body:
## Summary

Addresses the ACP `final_only` delivery-mode portion of #84486 â€” text produced before tool calls was lost because `resetTurnState()` cleared `finalOnlyOutputText` between model invocations within the same ACP turn.

Three changes in `final_only` mode:
- `finalOnlyOutputText` survives `resetTurnState()` across invocations
- `emittedOutputChars` and `truncationNoticeEmitted` also survive so `acp.stream.maxOutputChars` is enforced per accumulated turn
- Intermediate `done` events skip final text delivery â€” accumulated text is delivered once at turn completion via `flush(true)`

## Changes

- `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts`: `finalOnlyOutputText`, `emittedOutputChars`, and `truncationNoticeEmitted` no longer cleared in `resetTurnState()` for `final_only` mode; `flush()` accepts `skipFinalText` option; `onEvent` passes `skipFinalText: true` for `done`/`error` events in `final_only` mode
- `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.test.ts`: updated existing `final_only` buffering test; added regression test for multi-invocation text accumulation; added regression test for multi-invocation `maxOutputChars` truncation

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Pre-tool text in final_only delivery mode is discarded when resetTurnState() clears finalOnlyOutputText between model invocations. Output limit counters (emittedOutputChars) were also reset, allowing text to exceed acp.stream.maxOutputChars across invocations.

Real environment tested: macOS Darwin 25.4.0 (arm64), Node.js v24.14.1, OpenClaw 2026.5.19 (2cb5d72)

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts

pnpm format:check src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.test.ts

pnpm lint src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
```
 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  99 passed (99)
 Format: all matched files use the correct format
 Lint:   0 warnings and 0 errors
```

New regression tests:
- `accumulates text across multiple done events in final_only mode (#84486)` â€” verifies pre-tool text survives across invocations and is delivered together with post-tool text
- `enforces maxOutputChars across multiple invocations in final_only mode (#84486)` â€” verifies the per-turn output limit is enforced across multi-invocation turns (5-char limit, two invocations, only 5 chars delivered + truncation notice)

Observed result after fix: 99 tests pass (25 acp-projector including 2 new + 50 dispatch-acp + 24 dispatch-acp-delivery). Format and lint clean. Pre-tool text accumulates across model invocations and is delivered with post-tool text at turn completion. maxOutputChars is correctly enforced as a per-turn budget across all invocations.

What was not tested: Live Feishu/Telegram channel delivery with real ACP session (requires live credentials); the Feishu-specific streaming-card delivery path (separate from ACP dispatch)
